CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF AUG-12-2015 CONTINUED TO SEP-23-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, (1) DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION. NO OPPOSITION. (2) DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE STANDING. (3) DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE PURPORTED BREACH BY THE MOVING DEFENDANTS IS THE FAILURE TO "INFORM PLAINTIFF HER LOAN WAS SOLD, THUS DEPRAVING PLAINTIFF OF THE OPPORTUNITY TO NEGOTIATE WITH THE TRUE CREDITOR" (OPPOSITION 12:19-21). ASSUMING THE CONTRACTS RELIED UPON IN THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE THE NOTE AND THE DOT, IT IS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF DOT THAT "THE NOTE OR A PARTIAL INTEREST IN THE NOTE (TOGETHER WITH THIS SECURITY INSTRUMENT) MAY BE SOLD ONE OR MORE TIMES WITHOUT PRIOR NOTICE TO BORROWER." ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE AND DOES NOT PROPOSE TO ALLEGE THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT KNOW WHO WAS THE SERVICER OF THE NOTE/PURPORTED BENEFICIARY FOR THE PURPOSES OF NEGOTIATIONS. (4) DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE FORECLOSURE BY THE MOVING DEFENDANTS OR ANY ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND MOVING DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED.

CLERK'S NOTICE OF FILING OF NOTICE OF APPEAL

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS CTC REAL ESTATE SERVICES, RECONSTRUCT COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS ENTIRE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE IS DENIED AS PREMATURE. ON JANUARY 14, 2015 THE COURT ISSUED A TENTATIVE RULING SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER WITH 10 DAY LEAVE TO AMEND UNLESS AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED PRIOR TO THE HEARING. NO AMENDED COMPLAINT HAS BEEN FILED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADDRESS ALL ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY WAS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. NO ORDER HAS BEEN EXECUTED/ENTERED. NO NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER HAS BEEN FILED WITH THE COURT. ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: R. GOULDING; DEPT.501; NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
George Hill, Esq., appearing via COURT CALL (626-888-5202) of The Rodriguez Law Group for Plaintiff/Moving party Arketha Munir.
Alison Lippa , Esq., (310-315-8283) of McGuire Woods for Defendant/Opposing party Bank of American, Countrywide Home Loans Inc., CTC Real estate Services and Reconstruction Copmay N.A.

Plaintiff's counsel did not comply with 3.1038.
Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED without leave to amend
(1) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th and 6th causes of action. No opposition.
(2) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action. Plaintiff does not have standing.
(3) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action. The purported breach by the moving defendants is the failure to "inform Plaintiff her loan was sold, thus depraving Plaintiff of the opportunity to negotiate with the true creditor" (Opposition 12:19-21). Assuming the contracts relied upon in the 5th cause of action are the Note and the DOT, it is expressly provided in paragraph 20 of DOT that "The Note or a partial interest in the Note (together with this Security Instrument) may be sold one or more times without prior notice to Borrower." Additionally, Plaintiff does not allege and does not propose to allege that Plaintiff did not know who was the servicer of the Note/purported beneficiary for the purposes of negotiations. (4) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Plaintiff fails to allege foreclosure by the moving defendants or any actual controversy between Plaintiff and moving defendants. Plaintiff's request for Judicial Notice is DENIED. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Entire Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Alison V. Lopez, McGuire Woods LLP; 310-315-8200 for Defendants and moving party, CTC REAL ESTATE SERVICES, RECONSTRUCT COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC.'S
John Paul Pasaglia, 1-415-925-5886, specially appearing for plaintiff Aretha Munir.

The Court addressed the lack of the order regarding defendant's demurrer heard on January 15, 2015. Proposed orders presented by defendant discussed and an order signed on behalf of the Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay by the Honorable A. James Robertson II. The order was modified to reflect the tentative ruling as adopted at the January 15, 2015 hearing. The tentative ruling has been issued before 3:00 p.m. on January 14, 2015. At that time the Court was aware that Plaintiff submitted documents for electronic filing. However, the content and the nature of those documents were unknown, e.g. an amended complaint, opposition, or some other document. At 3:16 p.m., on January 14, 2015 the electronic filing appeared in the system. The filing was an opposition. Therefore, on January 15, 2015 the Court did not take the demurrer off calendar (as would have been done if an amended complaint had been filed prior to the hearing) and adopted the tentative ruling sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend. Order sustaining the demurrer signed in open Court and defendant instructed to serve it on all parties.
Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CTC REAL ESTATE SERVICES, RECONSTRUCT COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., COUNTRYWIDE HOME LOANS, INC.'S Motion to Dismiss Entire Action with Prejudice is DENIED as premature. On January 14, 2015 the Court issued a tentative ruling sustaining the demurrer with 10 day leave to amend unless an amended complaint was filed prior to the hearing. No amended complaint has been filed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend to address all issues raised by the demurrer. Prevailing party was to prepare an order. No order has been executed/entered. No notice of entry of order has been filed with the Court. Order is signed and filed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: R. Goulding; Dept.501; Not Reported
